title: Troop
description: Static definition for a troop type. See game-design/05-troop-stat-schema.md and game-design/08-troop-roster.md for the design rationale behind each field. Live per-unit state (currentHP, position, buffs) is NOT part of this schema — see TroopInstance in game-design/07-data-architecture.md.

domain.description: Which terrain/movement ruleset applies by default before per-unit overrides. "Land" means land vehicles specifically — Infantry is its own domain because Forest blocks Land vehicles but not Infantry, and Hills slow Infantry but not vehicles (see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md). Domain also doubles as a matchable key in damageDealtModifiers/damageReceivedModifiers, same as tags.

tags.description: Category/trait tags, e.g. ["Vehicle", "Tank", "Heavy"]. Referenced by other units' damage modifier dictionaries.

hp.description: Damage the unit can take before dying.

damage.description: Base damage per attack, before modifiers.

range.description: Max engagement distance. Separate from visionRange.

splashRadius.description: 0 = single-target; >0 damages a radius around impact.

visionRange.description: How far the unit can see. Kept separate from range.

speed.description: Base movement rate, in hexes per SECOND (see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md's Movement & Positioning section). Per-tick advancement is (speed / terrainCostMultiplier) * tickDuration — terrain cost divides effective speed down, it does not multiply it up.

maxSquadSize.description: Max troops of this type that can share one SquadInstance (see game-design/07-data-architecture.md). A freshly produced troop auto-joins the nearest same-type squad below this cap, or forms a new squad of 1. Set to 1 for troops that never merge into squads (Engineer, Commander, Disruptor).

maxSquadsLed.description: Only relevant for Commander-tagged troops: how many squads this Commander can lead as a regiment (baseline 4 — see game-design/04-combat.md).

commanderTier.description: Only relevant for Commander-tagged troops: which Command Centre level unlocks this Commander (basic at level 1, rare at level 2, best at level 3 — all lower tiers stay unlocked at higher levels). See game-design/02-bases-and-buildings.md's Command Centre & the Commander Cap section and data/buildings/schema.json's commanderProgression.

prioritizeStructures.description: Siege-behavior flag: units with this set default-target the nearest Structure (building/wall) over enemy troops when nothing is directly attacking them, instead of the normal nearest-enemy-troop default (see game-design/04-combat.md). Regular troops still need an explicit directed order to attack a non-defensive structure.

terrainOverrides.description: Per-unit exceptions layered on top of the Domain's default terrain rules.

canTarget.description: Domains/tags this unit is allowed to engage, independent of range. Empty array = cannot attack at all (e.g. Engineer, Glider, Disruptor). Reserved value "Structure" covers buildings AND walls uniformly (there is no separate "Building" vs "Wall" target kind) — most combat troops include it by default since sieging structures is core to the conquest win condition; Sniper is a deliberate exception that omits it (see game-design/08-troop-roster.md).

damageDealtModifiers.description: {tagOrDomain: multiplier} — bonus/penalty damage dealt vs. targets matching that tag or Domain value (or "Structure"). E.g. Grenadier -> {"Land": 1.5} applies only to Land-domain (vehicle) targets, not Infantry, Air, or Naval. Missing key defaults to 1.0.

damageReceivedModifiers.description: {tagOrDomain: multiplier} — bonus/penalty damage received from attackers matching that tag, Domain value, or damageType (see damageTypes below). Missing key defaults to 1.0.

damageTypes.description: Labels describing the nature of this unit's attack, matched against targets' damageReceivedModifiers exactly like a tag/Domain (e.g. "Fire" lets Flamethrower trigger a Wood Wall's {"Fire": 2.0} entry). Kept separate from descriptive Category/Trait tags so combat-relevant labels don't get mixed with matchmaking-only ones. Reserved value "Piercing" bypasses the target's damageReceivedModifiers entirely (armor-ignoring damage, e.g. Sniper) — it only cancels the target's received-side modifiers, not the attacker's own damageDealtModifiers. Splash is NOT a damage type — it's already fully expressed by splashRadius below.

revealRange.description: Distance at which a stealthed unit becomes visible to a non-detector. Required if stealth is true.

detector.description: Sees stealthed units at full visionRange, ignoring their revealRange.

revealsOnAttack.description: If true, attacking breaks this unit's own stealth until a few seconds pass without attacking.

cargoCapacity.description: How many units this troop can carry. 0 = cannot transport.

cargoAllowedTags.description: Domains/tags this unit is allowed to load as cargo, same mechanism as canTarget.

canLaunchCargoMidCombat.description: If true, cargo can be deployed mid-battle rather than only while idle/docked.

auras.description: Passive effects projected onto nearby units/buildings.

filter.description: Optional narrowing, e.g. a specific building type.

statusEffectOnHit.description: Temporary condition applied to the target on a successful hit, in addition to direct damage.

foodUpkeep.description: Ongoing Food consumption while the unit exists.

fuelUpkeep.description: Ongoing Fuel consumption; rules vary by Domain (see game-design/03-resources.md).

productionTime.description: Training duration, counted down in the producing building's own queue.
